title: SMBH Binary Candidate Catalog Entry (per paper)
description: One JSON file per paper, containing a paper-level header and a list of binary SMBH candidate entries. Each candidate records parameters extracted verbatim from the paper — no derived quantities beyond format-level unit conversions.

bibcode.description: ADS bibcode if available, otherwise a stable stem like the arXiv ID.

source_file.description: Path (relative to repo root) of the PDF or text used for extraction.

extracted_on.description: ISO-8601 date of the extraction run.

candidate_count.description: Number of distinct candidates recorded; must equal candidates.length.

errorType.description: See references/parameter_guide.md §Error types for the meaning and required error-field format of each.

value.description: Numeric value. Strings allowed only for rare cases (e.g. 'see Fig. 3'); prefer numbers.

error.description: Format depends on error_type. Gaussian: bare number. Lower/Upper limit: bare number (the limit itself). Two sided: string '(-x,+y)'. Range: bare number (half-width) or '(-x,+y)'. Assumed/Representative: null.

unit.description: Human-readable unit tag. The canonical unit for each field is fixed by the schema and parameter_guide.md — this is informational.

note.description: Optional per-field note, e.g. 'from M-sigma relation', 'derived from X-ray luminosity'.
Subcategory from references/parameter_guide.md §Evidence categories — must match the allowed notes for the chosen type.

source_standard_name.description: Primary formal name used for the source in the paper (e.g. 'SDSS J0159+0033', 'OJ 287', 'PG 1302-102').

source_nickname.description: Informal or paper-specific nickname, if different from the standard name.

ra_j2000.description: Right ascension, J2000. Sexagesimal (hh:mm:ss.s) or decimal degrees as a string.

dec_j2000.description: Declination, J2000. Sexagesimal (±dd:mm:ss) or decimal degrees as a string.

redshift.description: Cosmological redshift. Not an asterisked parameter — a plain number.

summary_notes.description: Brief general summary of the candidate or any idiosyncrasies in the analysis.

caveats.description: Points of uncertainty in the interpretation of the above parameters: mass provenance (dynamical vs scaling relation), figure-derived values, competing models, etc.

extension_project.description: Class-project ideas (undergrad or grad) scoped to a student following up on this paper.
